name: assert_fact
description: Assert a clinical fact into Lisa's working memory. Each fact has a type (e.g. gram, morphology) and a value. Facts about organisms or patients require an entity identifier. Use this to build up the clinical picture before running inference.

fact_type.description: The type of clinical fact to assert.

value.description: The value for this fact. Valid values depend on fact_type: gram=(pos|neg), morphology=(rod|coccus), aerobicity=(aerobic|anaerobic), growth-conformation=(clumps|chains), culture-site=(blood), burn=(serious), compromised-host=(t), culture-age=(integer as string), hospital-acquired=(t), recent-travel=(tropical), white-blood-count=(low), infection-site=(respiratory|abdominal).

entity.description: Identifier for the entity this fact describes (e.g. 'organism-1', 'patient-1'). Required for organism facts (gram, morphology, aerobicity, growth-conformation) and patient facts (burn, compromised-host, hospital-acquired, recent-travel, infection-site, white-blood-count). Not needed for culture-site or culture-age.

entity_class.description: The class of the entity: 'organism' or 'patient'. Defaults to 'organism' if omitted. Use 'patient' for burn, compromised-host, hospital-acquired, recent-travel, infection-site, and white-blood-count.

confidence.description: Confidence in this fact, from 0.0 to 1.0. Omit for full confidence (1.0). Use when the clinical evidence is uncertain (e.g. 'probably gram-positive' → 0.8).

name: run_inference
description: Trigger Lisa's forward-chaining inference engine. This fires all applicable rules against the current working memory and produces diagnostic conclusions with belief factors. Call this after asserting all available clinical facts.

name: get_conclusions
description: Retrieve diagnostic conclusions from the last inference run. Returns organism identities with their belief factors. Under certainty-factor mode, each belief is a single number in [-1, 1]. Under Dempster-Shafer mode, each belief is an object {bel, pl, ignorance} where bel is the lower bound of support, pl is the plausibility (upper bound), and ignorance is the width of the uncertainty interval (pl - bel). The response also includes a top-level belief_system field naming the active algebra.

name: get_rule_trace
description: Retrieve the rule trace from the last inference run. Shows which rules fired and in what order, providing an audit trail for the diagnostic reasoning. Use this to explain the reasoning behind conclusions.

name: get_partial_matches
description: Check which rules have some but not all conditions satisfied by current working memory. Returns rules that are 'close to firing' — showing which conditions are already met and which facts are still needed. Use this to determine what questions to ask the clinician next.

name: reset_session
description: Clear all facts from working memory and reset the session. Optionally switch the active belief system as part of the reset. Use this when starting a new patient case or when the clinician wants to start over.

belief_system.description: Optional: switch the active belief system on this reset. 'cf' = certainty factors (Shortliffe-Buchanan); 'ds' = simplified Dempster-Shafer. Omit to keep the current setting.
